title: Cyber Forensic Warm Up MCQ Set 2
subject: Cyber Forensic

questions.question: Which of the following is digital evidence rather than the physical electronic device containing it?
options: Browser-history records extracted from a laptop
questions.description: Digital evidence is the information stored or transmitted digitally. The hard disk, smartphone and USB drive are physical electronic evidence sources.

questions.question: Which finding is best classified as exculpatory evidence?
options: A malware sample found in the suspect's downloads
A log showing the suspect accessed the server
A deleted document containing stolen credentials
A verified record showing the suspect's account was disabled before the incident
questions.description: Exculpatory evidence supports innocence or reduces the likelihood that the person committed the alleged act.

questions.question: A laptop was deliberately used to create and send fraudulent invoices. What role did the laptop primarily play?
questions.description: An instrumentality is a device used to commit or facilitate an offence. The laptop was used as the means of committing fraud.

questions.question: An offender performs an unusual ritual after every intrusion, although the action is unnecessary for gaining access or hiding the attack. This behaviour is best described as:
questions.description: An offender signature is ritualised behaviour not required to complete the offence. Modus operandi refers to the practical method used to commit it.

questions.question: A file appears relevant to a case, but the examiner cannot demonstrate that it came from the claimed system or that it is what it claims to be. Which evidence quality is primarily missing?
questions.description: Authenticity establishes that evidence is genuine and originates from the claimed source.

questions.question: An investigator discovers that an earlier entry in the forensic notes contains an incorrect time. What is the most appropriate action?
options: Rewrite the entire page without mentioning the error
Leave the error unchanged and explain it only in court
Make a documented correction or appended entry with the date and initials
questions.description: Investigation notes should retain their chronological integrity. Corrections must remain visible and accountable rather than silently replacing earlier records.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of placing a seized internal hard disk in anti-static packaging?
options: To protect it from electrostatic discharge
To prevent its files from being indexed
questions.description: Anti-static packaging protects electronic components from damage caused by electrostatic discharge.

questions.question: Which copy should normally be used when an examiner wants to perform repeated tests using different forensic tools?
questions.description: Repeated examinations should be conducted on a working copy. The original evidence and master image should remain protected.

questions.question: Which characteristic most clearly distinguishes the E01 format from a basic RAW/DD image?
options: E01 can store case metadata and support compression and segmentation
RAW images cannot contain deleted data
RAW images automatically include examiner information
questions.description: E01 is a forensic container format that can include metadata, compression, checksums and segmentation. RAW is generally a direct bit-stream representation.

questions.question: An examiner acquires only files and folders exposed by the operating system, without copying unallocated sectors. Which acquisition type was performed?
questions.description: Logical acquisition collects files and logical objects available through the file system. It generally does not include all unallocated space and file slack.

questions.question: The recorded source hash and the completed image hash do not match. What should the investigator do?
options: Ignore the difference if the image opens correctly
Investigate the acquisition logs, algorithms, read errors and possible source changes
Change the image hash in the report to match the source
Conclude immediately that the suspect tampered with the image
questions.description: A mismatch means the compared data differs, but it does not identify the cause. Acquisition errors, bad sectors, source changes or incorrect hashing conditions must be investigated.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a Known File Filter?
options: To classify files by comparing their hashes with known hash sets
To capture running processes from memory
questions.description: A Known File Filter uses hash databases to identify known-good, known-bad or previously classified files.

questions.question: A stored forensic image changes over time because of unnoticed media degradation rather than intentional modification. This is an example of:
questions.description: Bit rot is gradual data corruption caused by storage-media degradation or related physical and electronic failures.

questions.question: Why might an examiner specify an image-fragment size while creating an image in FTK Imager?
options: To remove deleted files from the image
To convert physical acquisition into logical acquisition
To prevent the source device from being hashed
To divide the image into manageable segments
questions.description: Segmentation divides a large image into smaller files, which can simplify storage, transfer and compatibility with file-size limitations.

questions.question: Which hexadecimal sequence identifies the beginning of a PDF file?
questions.description: 25 50 44 46 represents %PDF, the standard PDF header.

questions.question: A .docx file begins with the bytes 50 4B 03 04. Why is this not necessarily suspicious?
options: Every Microsoft file begins with an executable header
The file has been converted into a PDF
DOCX uses the ZIP-based Office Open XML container format
The extension always overrides the file signature
questions.description: DOCX, XLSX and PPTX files are ZIP-based OOXML containers and commonly begin with the ZIP signature.

questions.question: In a modern Windows Recycle Bin, which artifact normally stores the original path, size and deletion metadata?
questions.description: The $I file contains deletion metadata. The corresponding $R file contains the deleted file's content.

questions.question: Which NTFS metadata file records whether clusters are allocated or free?
questions.description: $Bitmap maintains the allocation status of clusters on an NTFS volume.

questions.question: Which statement about an EXT-family inode is correct?
options: It stores the filename but not file metadata
It stores metadata and data-block references, while the filename is held in a directory entry
It is the EXT equivalent of a Windows drive letter
It exists only for deleted files
questions.description: An inode stores metadata and pointers to file data. The directory entry maps a filename to an inode number.

questions.question: Why can deleted-file recovery from an SSD be less successful than recovery from a traditional HDD?
options: SSDs do not use file systems
TRIM and garbage collection may remove data from released blocks
questions.description: TRIM informs the SSD that blocks are no longer required, and garbage collection may erase them before forensic recovery.

questions.question: Where are the main Windows system Registry hive files such as SAM, SYSTEM and SOFTWARE normally located?
questions.description: The principal system Registry hive files are stored under C:\Windows\System32\Config.

questions.question: Which Registry hive file is most directly associated with an individual user's preferences and activity?
questions.description: NTUSER.DAT stores user-specific Registry information and is loaded as the user's HKEY_CURRENT_USER hive.

questions.question: Which statement correctly distinguishes RegRipper from Regshot?
options: RegRipper captures RAM, while Regshot analyses disk images
RegRipper parses Registry hives, while Regshot compares Registry snapshots
RegRipper images disks, while Regshot verifies image hashes
RegRipper analyses packets, while Regshot extracts browser history
questions.description: RegRipper extracts artifacts from Registry hives using plugins. Regshot identifies Registry changes by comparing before-and-after snapshots.

questions.question: An investigator needs the vendor, product and serial number of a previously connected USB mass-storage device. Which Registry artifact is most directly relevant?
questions.description: USBSTOR commonly contains identification details for USB mass-storage devices, including vendor, product and serial information.

questions.question: When analysing an offline SYSTEM hive, how should an examiner determine which ControlSet00x was active?
options: Use CurrentControlSet, which exists permanently in the hive file
Use the most recently modified service key
Examine the Select key and its Current value
questions.description: CurrentControlSet is a live-system alias. In an offline hive, the SYSTEM\Select values identify the applicable numbered control set.

questions.question: Which Volatility 3 plugin provides operating-system, kernel and memory-layer information?
questions.description: windows.info provides foundational information about the memory image and the Windows system from which it was captured.

questions.question: A process appears in windows.psscan but not in windows.pslist. What is the most accurate interpretation?
options: The memory image contains no process information
It may be terminated or unlinked and requires further corroboration
questions.description: psscan searches memory for process objects and may find terminated or unlinked entries that are absent from the active linked list.

questions.question: Which Volatility plugin is most appropriate for identifying suspicious parent-child process relationships?
questions.description: windows.pstree organises processes according to their parent-child relationships.

questions.question: Which Volatility 3 plugin is used to locate network connections and socket artifacts in a Windows memory image?
questions.description: windows.netscan identifies network endpoints, addresses, ports, connection states and owning processes where available.

questions.question: What does windows.dumpfiles primarily extract?
options: Cached file objects located in memory
A complete executable image of every process
Registry passwords from the SAM hive
questions.description: windows.dumpfiles extracts supported cached file objects. It is not a universal process-memory dumping plugin.

questions.question: Which pairing correctly distinguishes memory acquisition from memory analysis?
options: Regshot acquires memory; Autopsy analyses it
Volatility acquires memory; FTK Imager analyses only Registry files
Comae Toolkit can acquire memory; Volatility can analyse the resulting image
RegRipper acquires memory; Sysinternals verifies it
questions.description: Tools such as Comae Toolkit, DumpIt or FTK Imager can capture RAM, while Volatility analyses memory-image contents.

questions.question: What is memory smear?
options: Encryption of a RAM image during acquisition
Inconsistency caused because memory contents change while they are being captured
Permanent physical damage to RAM chips
Removal of duplicate processes from a memory image
questions.description: A live system continues changing during acquisition, so different parts of the memory image may represent slightly different moments.

questions.question: Which Volatility plugin lists identified Registry hives together with their memory offsets and paths?
questions.description: hivelist lists identified Registry hives. hivescan searches memory for hive structures, while printkey displays keys and values.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of an ingest module in Autopsy?
options: To process a data source and extract or classify artifacts
To prevent a physical disk from being written to
To acquire volatile memory from a running system
questions.description: Ingest modules process the selected data source for files, hashes, metadata, keywords and other artifacts.

questions.question: Which type of information may be extracted from EXIF metadata in a photograph?
options: Camera model, capture time and possible GPS coordinates
questions.description: EXIF may contain camera details, timestamps, image properties and location information, depending on the device and file.

questions.question: Which Sysinternals utility is most appropriate for identifying files, Registry keys or other objects currently opened by a process?
questions.description: Handle displays open object handles associated with Windows processes.

questions.question: Which Linux log file commonly records failed login attempts?
questions.description: btmp records failed login attempts. wtmp generally records successful login and logout activity.

questions.question: Which mobile identifier is primarily associated with the physical handset?
questions.description: IMEI identifies mobile equipment. IMSI identifies the subscriber, while ICCID identifies the SIM card.

questions.question: Which item is specifically designed to isolate a mobile device from radio-frequency communication without relying on the device's software controls?
questions.description: A Faraday bag reduces or blocks radio communication, helping prevent remote access, synchronisation or wiping.

questions.question: Which mobile-acquisition method attempts to obtain the lowest-level available copy of device storage and may recover data unavailable through normal operating-system interfaces?
questions.description: Physical acquisition attempts to obtain lower-level storage data. Its success depends on the device, security controls, encryption and tool support.
